Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse (2023)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $381,593,754Details
International Box Office $309,230,984
Worldwide Box Office $690,824,738
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,312,561
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $12,661,953
Total $14,974,514
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Miles Morales returns for the next chapter of the Spider-Verse saga, an adventure that will transport Brooklyn’s full-time, friendly neighborhood Spider-Man across the Multiverse to join forces with Gwen Stacy and a new team of Spider-People to face off with a villain more powerful than anything they have ever encountered.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$120,663,589 (31.6% of total gross)
Legs:3.16 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:55.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$100,000,000 (worldwide box office is 6.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,313 opening theaters/4,332 max. theaters, 6.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $417,161,858

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 2nd, 2023 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
June 2nd, 2023 (IMAX) by Sony Pictures
January 19th, 2024 (IMAX) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: May 31st, 2023 (Wide) (France)
May 31st, 2023 (Wide) (Malaysia)
May 31st, 2023 (Wide) (Philippines)
May 31st, 2023 (Wide) (Indonesia)
May 31st, 2023 (Wide) (Finland)
Video Release: August 8th, 2023 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for sequences of animated action violence, some language and thematic elements.
(Rating bulletin 2784 (cert #53500), 5/24/2023)
Running Time: 140 minutes
Franchise: Spider-Man, Sony’s Marvel Universe
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead
Character Appearance: Spider-Man
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Filming location (country): United States
Filming location (state or county): California, United States
Geographical setting: Brooklyn, New York
Plot point: Alternative Dimensions / Parallel universe, Crime, Orphan, Secret Identity, Talking Animals
Social setting: Film Noir
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Source:Based on Comic/Graphic Novel
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Super Hero
Production/Financing Companies: Sony Pictures Animation, Columbia Pictures, Marvel Studios, Lord Miller, Pascal Pictures, Arad Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Miles Morales returns for the next chapter of the Spider-Verse saga, an adventure that will transport Brooklyn’s full-time, friendly neighborhood Spider-Man across the Multiverse to join forces with Gwen Stacy and a new team of Spider-People to face off with a villain more powerful than anything they have ever encountered.
